title: validation-framework: adversarial-twin benchmark run
description:
Per docs/VALIDATION_METRICS.md §6, an adversarial-twin parallel agent
is told the metric AND told to game it. If the twin's gains match a
candidate PR's, the candidate is presumed gamed and rejected.

## Why

The strongest Goodhart hedge: explicitly construct an adversary that
WILL find loopholes. If a candidate's improvement is reproducible by
a metric-aware adversary, it's almost certainly gaming, not science.
Anthropic / OpenAI 2024-2025 reward-hacking literature documents this
as the only effective defense against capable agents.

## Scope (large; deferred)

Requires: a separate orchestrator instance with a "game-the-metric"
profile, prompt-engineering work, and infrastructure for parallel
benchmark dispatch. Scope ~1 week of work.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] An adversarial profile in beefcake-swarm config/profiles.yaml
- [ ] An adversarial benchmark runner that reports per-metric gains
- [ ] PR-gate logic: if adversarial twin's gains overlap candidate's
  by > 50%, block merge
- [ ] Documented edge cases (what if both fail? what if adversarial
  also produces a real improvement?)
notes: swarm opened PR: https://github.com/TheFermiSea/CF-LIBS-improved/pull/88

title: Fix Performance Tests workflow: emit benchmark-results.json
description: .github/workflows/performance.yml run 24967840623 on commit 1a8cb03 fails the upload step with 'Cannot stat benchmark-results.json' — the upstream pytest-benchmark step exits without producing the expected JSON. Investigate the benchmark runner config (pytest --benchmark-json=benchmark-results.json), make sure the output path matches the upload step, or skip the upload step when benchmarks were not collected. Effort: small. No production impact, just removes a recurring red CI badge on main.

title: Fix Documentation workflow first-deploy: bootstrap gh-pages branch
description: .github/workflows/docs.yml fails on push to main because the gh-pages branch does not exist yet (run 24967840626 on commit 1a8cb03 errored 'Remote branch gh-pages not found in upstream origin'). Either (a) bootstrap an empty gh-pages branch, or (b) add force_orphan: true to the peaceiris/actions-gh-pages step so the first deploy creates it. Effort: small (one PR or one git push of an empty orphan branch). Affects no tests, but the failing badge is noise.

title: Auto-skip requires_rust + requires_db markers via conftest hook
description: tests/conftest.py:34 currently auto-skips requires_jax / requires_bayesian / requires_uncertainty when their dependency probe fails (closed CF-LIBS-improved-48c2). Extend the same probe pattern to requires_rust (probe: cflibs.native.cflibs_core or whatever the rust-bridge module is) and requires_db (probe: ASD_da/libs_production.db existing). Currently swarm/CI silently passes by excluding these markers in pytest filters; making the conftest the single source of truth removes the workflow drift. Effort: small. Acceptance: pytest tests/ with no extra flags skips Rust/DB tests cleanly when the deps are missing, runs them when present.

title: test_bayesian::TestTwoZoneMCMCSampler::test_smoke_run fails without NumPyro (missing requires_bayesian marker)
description:
Pre-existing failure on main. When NumPyro is not installed, TwoZoneMCMCSampler.__init__ raises ImportError('NumPyro required. Install with: pip install numpyro'). The test does not guard with a pytest.importorskip or the @requires_bayesian marker that similar Bayesian tests use, so it fails in environments without NumPyro.

Fix options:
1. Add pytestmark = pytest.mark.requires_bayesian at the TestTwoZoneMCMCSampler class level.
2. Guard with pytest.importorskip('numpyro') at the test-function or class level.
3. Make NumPyro a hard dependency of cflibs.inversion.solve.bayesian (not ideal — Bayesian is optional per pyproject.toml).

Reproduction:
  .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_bayesian.py::TestTwoZoneMCMCSampler::test_smoke_run

Error:
  ImportError: NumPyro required. Install with: pip install numpyro

This is not a regression from swarm/3fy3 — verified by running on main.
close_reason: Fixed on swarm/3fy3 @ b274b58. Added pytest_collection_modifyitems hook in tests/conftest.py that turns requires_jax/requires_bayesian/requires_uncertainty markers into actual auto-skip when the optional dep is missing. Test now reports SKIPPED instead of ImportError. Located + edited end-to-end via Serena MCP.

value: ADR-0001 implementation on integration branch feat/adr-0001-pattern-survey-impl. Per-bead specs: docs/adr/specs/T1-{1..6}-*.md. Runbook: docs/adr/ADR-0001-RUNBOOK.md. Cross-audit (2026-05-12) folded T2-3 + T2-6 into T1-1/T1-6; T2-4 retired; T2-2 narrowed to schema evolution. Wave-0 prerequisites (must merge first): 47hr baseline capture (P1), 30dq CI workflow fix (P1), oyup JAX 0.4.30+psutil pin (P2), od8m bd memory refresh (P2). Wave-1 (after wave-0): 5oar host/kernel split (absorbs T2-2 builder + T2-3 pytree registration; effort 6.5d) parallel with 14p6 lax.while_loop. Wave-2 SEQUENTIAL: e5o8 LDM broadening FIRST, then swgm forward-model unify (both touch spectrum_model.py L243-253 + batch_forward.py L395). Wave-3 parallel: ke4z chunked scan + 0mor Bayesian decomp. Subagent bootstrap sequence (runbook §3 step 0): bd memories adr-0001 → bd show <BEAD_ID> → mcp__serena__list_memories → read_memory architecture_and_data_flow. Worktree commands at .worktrees/<bead-id>/ with shared .venv symlink (forbid in-worktree pip). Sub-branches feat/adr-0001/<bead-id>-<short-name>. Docs refresh tracked by xph6.

CF-LIBS HARD PROJECT CONSTRAINT: the FINAL algorithm delivered by this project MUST be 100% physics-based — no neural networks, no trained ML models, no PLS/PCR, no learned embeddings, no sklearn/torch/tensorflow/keras/flax/equinox/transformers/jax.nn in the production code path. ML/LLMs are ONLY allowed in the optimization/search process itself (CodeEvolve, hierarchical ES, perturbation generation, council synthesis) — they propose candidate code, they never ship with it. The evaluator must AST-scan every evolved candidate against the blocklist and reject (fitness=-inf) any that imports ML libs. See epics 3fy3 and shv for full spec.

CF-LIBS PHYSICS OPTIMIZATION ROADMAP (literature-derived, all physics-only, no ML). Top 5 highest-leverage improvements ranked by (impact × inverse effort): (1) naf2 — inverse-variance weighting of Boltzmann fits by aki_uncertainty (small, immediate accuracy + uncertainty honesty across whole pipeline). (2) 1fcg — Pace 2025 doublet-ratio SA correction as a cheap second-opinion to CDSB. (3) wzus — min >=3 lines + per-element Boltzmann R^2 gate to push identification precision past 0.7 (closes the precision regression in shv epic). (4) t2tj — joint multi-gate Saha-Boltzmann fit; reuses existing runtime/temporal infrastructure for major composition-uncertainty reduction. (5) mgp5 — Hermann two-region (core+corona) forward model; large effort but biggest expected lift on inhomogeneous matrices (slag/glass/silicate). Open frontiers per Pedarnig 2023 JAAS review: plasma inhomogeneity (covered by mgp5) and Bayesian nuisance marginalization (covered by 4w8x). Field is in a refinement phase — no pure-physics ML-free foundational solver paper appeared 2022-2026, so progress comes from orthogonal accuracy gains stacked together. All these block the shv accuracy epic.
